title: Veterinary (Licensing of Artificial Insemination Technicians) Regulations, 2011
title_en: Veterinary (Licensing of Artificial Insemination Technicians) Regulations, 2011

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Veterinary (Licensing of Artificial Insemination Technicians) Regulations, 2011.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations unless the context otherwise requires:— “ Act ” means the Veterinary Act; “ Artificial insemination technician ” means a licensed Veterinarian or licensed enrolled or enlisted paraprofessional designated as an inserminator and includes any person who hold a certificate in artificial insemination that is recognised by the Council ; “ Certificate of compentence ” means a certificate awarded to a trainee at the completion of formal training in Artificial Insemination; “ Council ” means the Veterinary Council of Tanzania established under section 3 of the Act ; “ License ” means the certificate issued to the Artificial insemination technician under these regulations; “ Minister ” means the Minister responsible for Livestock Development. Part II – Licensing of Artificial insemination technicians

provisions.title: Qualification for licensing insemination technicians
provisions.content: A person who intends to practice as an artificial insemination technician shall apply for a license to the Council he shall be:— (a) a person of good mental and moral character; (b) a registered veterinarian or an enrolled paraprofessional or an enlisted Paraprofessional Assistant or a person who has received training and has been awarded a certificate of competence in artificial insemination recognized by the Council ; (c) a citizen of Tanzania; (d) a person who has not committed ay offence or acts prohibited under the Act . [Please note: numbering as in original.] (2) The Minister shall, on the recommendation of the Council , publish in the gazette other equivalent qualification which entitle the holder for licensing in terms of these regulations. (3) a foreigner may be entitled for licensing as an artificial insemination technician if:— (a) intends to undertake a special work for which no Tanzanian is qualified; and (b) has passed an examination administered to him by the Council for that purpose.

provisions.title: Procedure for licensing
provisions.content: (1) Any person who intends to be licensed as an artificial insemination Technician shall submit his application in the prescribed form set out the First Schedule and such application shall be accompanied by evidence of qualifications and application fee of Tanzania shillings five thousand shillings in case of a Tanzanian and one hundred US Dollars in case of a foreigner. (2) The Registrar shall, after the receipt of the application under these regulations submit such application to the Council for final determination. (3) The Council may, in determining the application submitted to it matter under sub-regulation (2) , direct the Registrar:— (a) to issue the licence as prescribed under these regulations after paying a fee often thousand shillings and in case of a foreigner two hundred US Dollars; (b) refuse to license and give reasons thereon.

provisions.title: Retention fee
provisions.content: (1) There shall be a Retention fee of five thousands shillings to be paid annually by a licensed artificial insemination technician who is Tanzanian and in case of foreigner one hundred US Dollars. (2) A Licensed Registered Veterinarian, enrolled Paraprofessional and enlisted Paraprofessional Assistant shall be exempted from payment of retention fees.

provisions.title: List of licensed Artificial inseminations technician
provisions.content: (1) The Council shall cause to be kept the list of licensed Artificial insemination technician to whom the Council has granted licence under these regulations. (2) The list kept under sub-regulation (1) shall contain:— (a) names, qualifications, place where qualifications was obtained, date of obtaining the qualifications and any other personal data of the licensed Artificial insemination technicians, which shall be determined from time to time by the council; (b) an address where licensed Artificial insemination technician can be contacted; (c) particulars of licensing including area of operation and date of first commencement.

provisions.title: Publication of licensed Artificial insemination technicians
provisions.content: The Minister shall, on the acommendation of the Council and by notice in the Gazette publish names, qualifications and address of licensed artificial insemination technicians. Part III – Offences and penalties

provisions.title: Offences and penalties
provisions.content: (1) Any artificial insemination technician to who:— (a) make untruthful of deceitful or misleading reports or; (b) conduct himself dishonestly or unfairly; or (c) unethically signs or validates a report without actually or physically carrying out the analysis; commits an offence. (2) Any artificial insemination technician who gives false any information or which he reasonably believe to be untrue, commits an offence and shall on conviction be liable to a fine not exceeding five hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months or to both. (3) Any person who contravenes any provision of these Regulations where penalty is not specifically provided commits an offence and shall upon conviction be liable to a fine not exceeding five hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months or to both such fine and imprisonment. First Schedule Application for licensing Artificial insemination technician [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Second Schedule Licence for Artificial insemination technician [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced.
